As a result of the general inventory count conducted during the first and second shifts for item 10169, the pallets with LPNs R221216 and R227714 were identified as missing.
After completing the corresponding verification, it was determined that both pallets were located in the Staging area; however, they had been recorded as consumed in the production log without the corresponding transaction being entered into the system. Therefore, the necessary inventory adjustment will be processed to reconcile the records.
This situation highlights the importance of ensuring that all labels and inventory movements are recorded accurately and in a timely manner within the system. Compliance with this process is essential to maintain inventory accuracy, ensure material traceability, and minimize unnecessary adjustments and inventory discrepancies. Rich Brierley Marcos Montano Alan Greer Rose Smuin Chad Stone

Good morning all,

Just want to make sure on ML1 only we do not have to wait for multiple tempers when we are running continuously and below 1.0. 

If you get a high temper of 0.8 or 0.9 and have to stop and then get a 0.7 or below you can continue running. 

You should still follow all other standards in case you’re down for a longer period of time. 

Please come back with any questions you have.

Grey Boscan

Please do a reheat and let’s start fresh
Grey Boscan I think at this point we should drop Crystalization. Let's go down to 79.5.
We are now in reheat mode
Nevermind.
When remelt finishes and the MK plain is loaded it will be at 83.8 on mixing water. Don't let it wait to temper at 83.8, just drop it to 82 right after loading the recipe.

You have to set the "tunnel 1 belt speed" to 1.2 (as per centerline) to be able to get 47% in the "belt thickness HMI" while having the "sprinkler roller" at 9.0% (as per centerline). If the "tunnel 1 belt speed" is set to 1.0, you will get 39% in the "belt thickness HMI" even if the "sprinkler roller" is set to 9.0%. William, Justin, Nathan.

Freddy I reduced the target weight by 5g and haven't seen a reject for over 5 minutes. The pieces are now on the heavier side, but still within target. Hi Team,

With the installation of Sollich 4 Kitchen, Volker (Chocotech) recommended that whenever a Kitchen is shut down for more than one day, we should close the steam valves and completely drain the line.

If everyone agrees, we can implement this practice in all Kitchens to help protect the equipment and reduce potential issues during extended downtime.

At the moment, I can perform this procedure in Sollich 2 Kitchen. Please let me know if you approve moving forward.

Thank you.
Curt Willis Justin Garner

To clarify, if the line stops on Saturday and will run again on Monday, there is no need to shut off the steam. However, if the line stops on Friday and will restart on Monday, it would be recommended to shut off the steam. For example, right now with Sollich 2 Kitchen, which is currently down.

2 barrels of chocolate that arrived from L3 yesterday can not be used. I noticed that there were orange tags and empty tags on the barrels, which means we used a scrap barrel for clean chocolate.  It will have to be scrapped. Justin Garner Curt Willis Dakota Adair Jody Howse

Since raising the speed we’ve ran into a few challenges: 

1. Congestion at the box-belt, which has then lead to a build up of packed boxes needing to be dated and taped. 

The speed gauge for this belt is broken, however, even if we raise the speed this will affect the print.


2. Piece count being lower than 24-21 pieces. 
Since raising the speed we’ve seen that number go down to 17-19

We’ve increased the rotation of  bark breaker, and will post back if that resolves the matter. 

3. Due to the congestion at the box belt we started getting accumulation at the check weigher. 

We have two carts of accumulated pouches. 


We paused at 8:15 to try and work the accumulation down, and will resume at 8:45.
We are going to edit the lot numbers for the pallets that have already been created and then re-label all the pallets in Springville. Paula Pena
We have 22 acceptable items in each bag, and the bag weighs 149, which is within the target range.
